identify_cve.expected_solution: Agent must identify the CVE as CVE-2021-23337 (GHSA-35jh-r3h4-6jhm) affecting the npm package `lodash` at versions < 4.17.21, fixed in 4.17.21. The BLAST_RADIUS.md report should name the CVE identifier explicitly and the vulnerable version range.
evaluation_criteria: Names the CVE identifier CVE-2021-23337 verbatim
Cites the vulnerable version range < 4.17.21 (and/or fixed version 4.17.21)

find_direct_deps.expected_solution: Agent must enumerate the direct dependents of `lodash` inside the workspace repos. Per ground_truth, these are: webpack, jest, and @babel/traverse. The answer must reference each by package name and cite the manifest file (e.g. package.json, go.mod, requirements.txt, pom.xml) where the dependency is declared.
evaluation_criteria: Lists each ground-truth direct dependent: webpack, jest, and @babel/traverse
References the manifest file(s) where the dependency is declared (package.json / go.mod / requirements.txt / pom.xml / Cargo.toml as applicable)
Distinguishes runtime vs dev/test dependencies for `lodash` where the manifest type indicates it

trace_transitive_paths.expected_solution: Agent must trace transitive dependency paths through intermediaries. Per ground_truth: lodash -> webpack -> create-react-app; lodash -> jest -> create-react-app; lodash -> @babel/traverse -> babel-loader -> webpack. The dependency_chain detail is: lodash -> webpack (devDependency) via package.json; affected_versions=< 4.17.21; lodash -> jest (dependency (via jest-haste-map)) via packages/jest-haste-map/package.json; affected_versions=< 4.17.21. The answer must show at least one A -> B -> C path that explains how a downstream consumer pulls in `lodash` indirectly.
evaluation_criteria: Documents at least one transitive path of the form A -> B -> C through `lodash`
Names every intermediary package present in the ground_truth transitive_paths (@babel/traverse, babel-loader, jest, and webpack)
Distinguishes direct from transitive dependents in the report

version_analysis.expected_solution: Agent must classify each consumer as affected or unaffected based on the resolved/pinned `lodash` version. The vulnerable range is < 4.17.21; the fix landed in 4.17.21. The answer must explicitly call out the version each consumer pins (from package-lock.json / yarn.lock / go.sum / Pipfile.lock / Cargo.lock as applicable) and decide affected vs not.
evaluation_criteria: States the vulnerable range < 4.17.21 and the fixed version 4.17.21
Classifies at least one consumer as affected or not affected based on its pinned/resolved version
References a lockfile or resolution manifest as the source of the pinned version
